content:
📌 LEVEL 1 (ENTRIES & DRAFTS)

✅ Letter 1 = Same as first letter from the last snapshot.
✅ Letter 2 = Same as second letter from the last snapshot.
✅ Letter 3 = Same as third letter from the last snapshot.
✅ Adds -01, -02, -03, etc., for iterations.
✅ Level 1 does NOT affect the universal numbering system.

📌 Examples:

ZCD.203-01 → ZCD.203-02
PQS.113-01 → PQS.113-02
FGI.049-01 → FGI.049-02
BCE.019-01 → BCE.019-02

📌 SNAPSHOT SYSTEM

✅ Each module maintains its own snapshot, storing the latest assigned letter values.
✅ A snapshot records:
• Letter 1, 2, and 3 of the most recent entry.
• The universal number at the most recent entry.
✅ When moving from one level to another, it uses the latest snapshot to assign letters.

📌 Example Snapshot:
context: From document: 📜 FINALIZED RULES FOR THE NAMING STRUCTURE & UNIVERSAL SYSTEM — chunk 4 (chunk 4/6)
